name: v0.14.0 — FHIR R4 Healthcare Scheduling via Medplum + Epic
body:
Healthcare appointment scheduling via a clean FHIR R4 interface backed by Medplum, with optional Epic STU3 proxy for production EMR write-back.

## Why This Feature Exists

Healthcare is one of the highest-value verticals for AI-assisted engagement. Patients routinely contact providers to book, reschedule, or ask about appointments — interactions that today require a human agent or a fragmented self-service portal. This release makes Kira a first-class participant in that workflow.

**The core problem**: Clinical scheduling lives inside Epic (and other EMRs). AI agents that can't read or write to the EMR are limited to collecting information and handing off to humans — they cannot close the loop. Every handoff is a drop-off risk, a delay for the patient, and unnecessary cost for the provider.

**What this unlocks**:
- Kira's AI agents can now find available appointment slots and book them directly inside Epic, in real time, during a voice call or chat conversation — with no human in the loop for routine bookings.
- Providers connecting Kira to their Epic instance immediately gain AI-assisted scheduling across every channel (voice, web chat, SMS) without rebuilding their scheduling infrastructure.
- The FHIR R4 interface is standards-based: any future EMR integration (Cerner, Athena, etc.) slots in behind the same API surface, protecting the investment.

**Business impact**:
- **Deflection at scale** — routine appointment requests (the majority of inbound contact centre volume in healthcare) can be fully automated, reducing cost-per-interaction significantly.
- **After-hours coverage** — AI agents handle booking outside clinic hours without on-call staff, increasing patient satisfaction and captured appointments.
- **Referral completion** — the `basedOn`/`incomingReferral` mapping means agents can act on referral orders directly, closing a loop that today has a ~30% drop-out rate due to patient friction.
- **Compliance-ready from day one** — HIPAA minimum-necessary access (FGA), immutable audit trails, AES-GCM credential encryption, and PHI-safe operating rules are built in, not retrofitted.
- **Integration, not replacement** — Epic remains the system of record. Kira is the system of engagement. No clinical data is duplicated; every booking is confirmed in the EMR immediately.

## New Features

### FHIR R4 Facade
- `POST /v1/fhir/Appointment/$find` — search available slots (R4 in, R4 Bundle out)
- `POST /v1/fhir/Appointment/$book` — book a slot (R4 in, booked R4 Appointment out)
- `GET /v1/fhir/metadata` — custom CapabilityStatement advertising Epic proxy support
- Epic STU3 proxy: R4↔STU3 field mapping (`reasonCode`→`reason`, `basedOn`→`incomingReferral`, `comment` field)
- FNV-1a idempotency key on `$book`; duplicate requests return existing appointment
- HIPAA-compliant FGA patient-access check on `$find` and `$book` with 403 + audit on denial

### Medplum Bots
- `appointment-find-bot.ts` — R4 params → Epic STU3 `$find` → R4 Bundle
- `appointment-book-bot.ts` — R4+slot → Epic STU3 `$book` → stored R4 Appointment
- `appointment-mapper.ts` — bidirectional R4↔STU3 conversion
- `epic-oauth-client.ts` — OAuth2 with RS384 JWT assertion (RFC 7523) for production, `client_credentials` sandbox fallback
- `withRetry` with safe `shouldRetry` predicate: GET retries 429+503, POST retries 429/network-only (no double-booking)
- In-flight token deduplication; 10s `AbortSignal.timeout` on all Epic calls

### EHR CLI (`kira ehr`)
- `kira ehr check` — 11-step preflight (API, config, Medplum URL/creds/connectivity, Epic URL/creds/connectivity, Bot IDs, last test status). Runs without prior login.
- `kira ehr config set/show/test` — per-tenant Medplum + Epic credential management
- `kira ehr bot deploy` — deploys bots and writes `find_bot_id`/`book_bot_id` back to D1
- `kira ehr patient get/create`, `kira ehr appointment find/book/list`
- All user-supplied args via `execFileSync` (array args) — no shell injection risk

### AI Agent MCP Tools (7 tools)
- `fhir_appointment_find`, `fhir_appointment_book`
- `fhir_patient_get`, `fhir_patient_create`
- `fhir_appointment_list`, `fhir_communication_log`, `fhir_metadata`

### EHR Settings UI
- `Settings → EHR / FHIR` — tenant Medplum + Epic credential configuration
- Dirty-secret tracking: only modified secrets sent on save (no masked-value overwrite)
- Test-before-save: test button uses current form values without updating saved state
- Epic sandbox toggle pre-fills URLs; private key file upload for RFC 7523

## Security Hardening
- AES-GCM encryption for all stored Medplum/Epic secrets (EHR-specific PBKDF2 salt)
- Epic client secret removed from Bot execution payload — route exchanges OAuth and passes short-lived Bearer token only
- Patient reference validated against FHIR ID regex before Medplum query (path traversal fix)
- Typed `HttpError` class; retry logic uses `instanceof` not string matching
- Module-level Epic token cache with tenant isolation and retry-on-401 invalidation

## Infrastructure
- D1 migration `112_medplum_configs.sql` — SQLite-compatible (no PL/pgSQL)
- D1 migration `113_epic_private_key.sql` — encrypted RSA private key storage
- Docker Compose for local Medplum dev (`docker/medplum/`)
- Zod validation on `$find`/`$book` with FHIR R4 `OperationOutcome` 400 responses

## Tests
- 68 Medplum bot tests (TDD: written before implementation)
- 1793 API worker tests (FHIR routes unskipped, FGA tests added)
- 4,075 total tests passing across all packages

## Docs
- [`docs/features/medplum-fhir-facade.md`](docs/features/medplum-fhir-facade.md) — architecture, setup guide, API examples, CLI reference

## Issues Closed
